message:
feat(ui): animate spectral background film grain with oscillating frequency

The global SpectralBackground component previously rendered a static
SVG feTurbulence noise filter for the film-grain overlay. While
effective, the perfectly static grain felt lifeless compared to the
rest of the site's animated aesthetic.

This commit adds a subtle breathing effect to the grain:
  - Inserts an SVG <animate> element inside the <feTurbulence> filter
    that oscillates baseFrequency between 0.65 and 0.68 over a 2-second
    cycle (repeatCount="indefinite"). This creates a barely perceptible
    shimmer that makes the grain feel organic without being distracting.
  - Bumps the grain layer opacity from 0.03 to 0.04 to make the
    texture slightly more present, complementing the animation.
  - Adjusts the SVG structure: the self-closing <feTurbulence /> tag
    becomes an open/close pair to contain the nested <animate> child.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -17,15 +17,22 @@ export default function SpectralBackground() {
   return (
     <div className="fixed inset-0 z-[-5] pointer-events-none overflow-hidden select-none">
       {/* Film Grain / Static Noise */}
-      <div className="absolute inset-0 opacity-[0.03] mix-blend-overlay">
+      <div className="absolute inset-0 opacity-[0.04] mix-blend-overlay">
         <svg viewBox="0 0 200 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" className="w-full h-full">
           <filter id="noiseFilter">
             <feTurbulence 
               type="fractalNoise" 
               baseFrequency="0.65" 
               numOctaves="3" 
               stitchTiles="stitch" 
-            />
+            >
+              <animate 
+                attributeName="baseFrequency" 
+                values="0.65;0.68;0.65" 
+                dur="2s" 
+                repeatCount="indefinite" 
+              />
+            </feTurbulence>
           </filter>
           <rect width="100%" height="100%" filter="url(#noiseFilter)" />
         </svg>
